reason: Per the PitchGPT methodology paper §5.3, the LSTM baseline is retrained from scratch in each run of scripts/pitchgpt_2025_holdout.py and no persistent LSTM checkpoint is committed to models/. The Phase 2 post-game synthesis spec hard-rules forbid retraining ('DO NOT retrain any model. Use committed checkpoints.'). Therefore an apples-to-apples LSTM replay on the 320 live pitches is not reproducible from committed-state-only sources right now.

live_log_loss_pitchgpt_typeonly.note: Mean log-loss from the live logger over the 17-class pitch-type marginal (NOT comparable to the 2210-token composite the methodology paper uses). Recorded only for completeness.

reference_2025_oos_edge.verdict_per_paper: FAIL by 1.2pp on point estimate (CI upper bound 15.51 reaches spec). This is the established baseline; nothing in the live game can move it on n=320.

honest_framing_for_live_game: n=320 single-game pitches gives a bootstrap CI on any one-game perplexity statistic that is wider than the headline 13.80% edge by construction (sqrt(53723/320) ~13x more variance), so even with an LSTM checkpoint, a single-game perplexity comparison would not confirm or refute the OOS edge. The right one-game test is calibration stability (reliability_diagram.png + reliability_data.json), not a perplexity replay.
recommended_followup: Commit a frozen LSTM checkpoint (models/pitch_lstm_v1.pt) generated by a one-time run of pitchgpt_2025_holdout.py with --epochs 5 --seed 42, so future live-game case studies can do the apples-to-apples replay without retraining.
Aggregate >= 5-10 live-game logs before running a perplexity comparison; single-game n is too small to test the 13.80% edge.
